steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the slope of the line $42x - 6y = -28$. Convert to slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$ and read the coefficient of $x$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
Isolate $y$: divide $-6y = -42x - 28$ by $-6$ to get $y = 7x + \tfrac{14}{3}$. The slope is $7$.
  • Choice A — -7: wrong sign on the $x$-coefficient. ✗
  • Choice B — $-\frac{3}{2}$: used the ratio of the $x$-coefficient to the constant instead of isolating $y$. ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{3}{2}$: used that same ratio but flipped the sign. ✗
  • Choice D — 7: the coefficient of $x$ after isolating $y$. ✓
That is the correct answer.
